---
title: "Core Concepts"
---

`qp` works best when you treat `qp.yaml` as your repo's operational contract: tasks define what can run, and metadata (guards, scopes, vars, profiles, secrets, context) defines how safely and predictably it runs for both humans and agents.

## Glossary

| Concept | What it means | 3-line YAML example | Learn more |
|---|---|---|---|
| Task | A runnable unit of work (`cmd`, `steps`, or `run`). | `tasks:`<br>`  test:`<br>`    cmd: go test ./...` | [Tasks and Params](tasks-and-params.qmd) |
| Guard | A named validation bundle that runs all steps and reports combined health. | `guards:`<br>`  ci:`<br>`    steps: [lint, test]` | [Getting Started](getting-started.qmd) |
| Scope | A named set of paths used for context, planning, and focused workflows. | `scopes:`<br>`  backend:`<br>`    paths: ["cmd/**", "internal/**"]` | [Home](index.qmd) |
| Group | A way to organize tasks in `qp list` and docs for discoverability. | `groups:`<br>`  quality:`<br>`    tasks: [lint, test]` | [Home](index.qmd) |
| Alias | A short alternate name for a task. | `tasks:`<br>`  build:`<br>`    aliases: [b]` | [Tasks and Params](tasks-and-params.qmd) |
| Variable | A reusable value available via `{{vars.name}}` and expression helpers. | `vars:`<br>`  region: eu-west-1`<br>`  image: app:latest` | [Vars, Templates, Profiles, Secrets](vars-templates-profiles-secrets.qmd) |
| Template | Reusable snippets or parameterized task blueprints expanded at load time. | `templates:`<br>`  build_cmd: go build ./...`<br>`  # used as {{template.build_cmd}}` | [Vars, Templates, Profiles, Secrets](vars-templates-profiles-secrets.qmd) |
| Profile | An environment overlay selected by `QP_PROFILE` or `--profile`. | `profiles:`<br>`  prod:`<br>`    vars: { region: us-east-1 }` | [Vars, Templates, Profiles, Secrets](vars-templates-profiles-secrets.qmd) |
| Secret | A value loaded from env/file and redacted from output/events/cache. | `secrets:`<br>`  api_key:`<br>`    from: env` | [Vars, Templates, Profiles, Secrets](vars-templates-profiles-secrets.qmd) |
| Codemap | Semantic project metadata for conventions, domains, and architecture intent. | `codemap:`<br>`  layers: [api, service, data]`<br>`  glossary: []` | [Home](index.qmd) |
| Context | A bounded repository briefing for agent or human handoff. | `context:`<br>`  default_mode: agent`<br>`  max_tokens: 4000` | [Home](index.qmd) |
| Plan | A pre-edit execution/intention summary of work likely to change. | `tasks:`<br>`  plan:`<br>`    cmd: qp plan --json` | [Events and JSON Output](events-json.qmd) |
| Repair | A diagnostic workflow that turns failed checks into actionable guidance. | `tasks:`<br>`  repair:`<br>`    cmd: qp repair` | [Events and JSON Output](events-json.qmd) |

## How The Concepts Fit Together

A realistic `qp` loop usually looks like this:

1. Define **tasks** and group them with **aliases/groups** so `qp list` and `qp help` stay understandable.
2. Add **vars**, **templates**, **profiles**, and **secrets** to keep command definitions clean and environment-specific.
3. Use **run expressions** and/or pipeline steps to compose tasks into a deterministic workflow.
4. Capture quality gates with **guards** and feed structured output to CI with `--json` and `--events`.
5. Use **scopes**, **context**, **codemap**, **plan**, and **repair** to make agent-assisted change loops reliable.

### End-to-end mini example

```yaml
project: payments
default: check

vars:
  image: ghcr.io/acme/payments:{{vars.git_sha}}
  git_sha:
    sh: git rev-parse --short HEAD

profiles:
  prod:
    vars:
      image: ghcr.io/acme/payments:stable

tasks:
  lint:
    desc: Lint Go code
    cmd: golangci-lint run

  test:
    desc: Run tests
    cmd: go test ./...

  build:
    desc: Build container
    cmd: docker build -t {{vars.image}} .

  check:
    desc: Local quality gate
    run: par(lint, test) -> build
```

```bash
qp check
QP_PROFILE=prod qp check --json
```

The first run uses repo-derived vars; the second overlays production values and emits machine-readable output.

## Status And Exit Code Contract

`qp` reports a task/guard status and uses process exit codes that scripts and CI can rely on.

| Status | Meaning | Typical exit code |
|---|---|---|
| `pass` | Task/guard completed successfully. | `0` |
| `fail` | Command error, validation failure, or unmet condition that marks failure. | non-zero from command (commonly `1`) |
| `cancelled` | Execution stopped by user signal/interruption before completion. | `130` (SIGINT-style) |
| `timeout` | Task exceeded configured timeout. | `124` |

When you use `--json`, these values appear directly in the structured result payload so downstream tooling does not need to parse terminal text.
